title: Replit’s Amjad Masad on the Cursor deal, fighting Apple, and why he’d rather not sell
description: At TechCrunch's sold-out StrictlyVC event in San Francisco on Thursday night, we covered a lot of ground in a short time, beginning with the question everyone in the industry is asking right now: in a world where rival Cursor is reportedly in talks to be acquired by SpaceX for $60 billion, is Replit also bound to sell?
descriptionDE: Bei der ausverkauften StrictlyVC-Veranstaltung von TechCrunch in San Francisco am Donnerstagabend haben wir in kurzer Zeit viel erreicht, beginnend mit der Frage, die sich derzeit jeder in der Branche stellt: In einer Welt, in der sich der Rivale Cursor Berichten zufolge in Gesprächen über die Übernahme durch SpaceX für 60 Milliarden US-Dollar befindet, ist Replit...
titleDE: Amjad Masad von Replit über den Cursor-Deal, den Kampf gegen Apple und warum er lieber nicht verkaufen möchte

Along the way, he warned that AI could destroy us all and sat through…

Unterwegs warnte er, dass KI uns alle zerstören könnte und saß da...
titleDE: Musk vs. Altman Woche 1: Elon Musk sagt, er sei betrogen worden, warnt davor, dass KI uns alle töten könnte, und gibt zu, dass xAI die Modelle von OpenAI destilliert

title: All the evidence revealed so far in Musk v. Altman
description:
The Musk v. Altman trial is underway, and that means exhibits, or the evidence to be presented in court, are being revealed piece by piece. So far, email exchanges, photos, and corporate documents are circulating from the earliest days of OpenAI - and from before the AI lab even had a name. Some high-level takeaways: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang gave OpenAI an in-demand supercomputer, Musk largely drafted OpenAI's mission and heavily influenced its early structure, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman appeared to want to lean heavily on Y Combinator for early support for OpenAI, OpenAI president Greg Brockman and Ilya Sutskever worried about Musk's level of con …
Read the full story at The Verge.
descriptionDE: Der Prozess Musk gegen Altman ist im Gange, und das bedeutet, dass Beweisstücke oder Beweise, die vor Gericht vorgelegt werden sollen, Stück für Stück enthüllt werden. Bisher kursieren E-Mail-Austausch, Fotos und Unternehmensdokumente aus den Anfängen von OpenAI – und aus der Zeit, bevor das KI-Labor überhaupt einen Namen hatte. Einige hoch...
titleDE: Alle bisher im Fall Musk gegen Altman enthüllten Beweise

description: Cybersecurity was already under strain before AI entered the stack. Now, as AI expands the attack surface and adds new complexity, the limits of legacy approaches are becoming harder to ignore. This session from MIT Technology Review’s EmTech AI conference explores why security must be rethought with AI at its core, not layered on after…

Da KI nun die Angriffsfläche erweitert und neue Komplexität hinzufügt, werden die Grenzen veralteter Ansätze immer schwerer zu ignorieren. In dieser Sitzung der EmTech AI-Konferenz des MIT Technology Review geht es darum, warum Sicherheit neu gedacht werden muss ...

title: Pentagon strikes classified AI deals with OpenAI, Google, and Nvidia — but not Anthropic
description:
The Pentagon has struck deals with OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, Elon Musk's xAI, and the startup Reflection, allowing the agency to use their AI tools in classified settings, according to an announcement on Friday. At the same time, the Defense Department has left out Anthropic - which it previously used for classified information - after declaring it a supply-chain risk.
This builds upon deals with OpenAI and xAI, which have already reached agreements with the Pentagon for the "lawful" use of their AI systems. A report from The Information suggests Google has struck a similar agreement. As noted by The Wall Street Journal, Mi …
Read the full story at The Verge.
descriptionDE: Laut einer Ankündigung vom Freitag hat das Pentagon Verträge mit OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, Elon Musks xAI und dem Startup Reflection abgeschlossen, die es der Behörde ermöglichen, ihre KI-Tools in geheimen Umgebungen einzusetzen. Gleichzeitig hat das Verteidigungsministerium Anthropic weggelassen – was...

title: Elon Musk had a bad week in court
description:
Elon Musk is the one who wanted this trial. He has spent months claiming OpenAI "stole a nonprofit," and saying he was the actual driving force behind one of the most important companies currently in tech. All indications are that he won't win his case against the company, but he's fighting it anyway. So you'd think he'd have done better when it was his time to take the stand.

Verge subscribers, don't forget you get exclusive access to ad-free Vergecast wherever you get your podcasts. Head here. Not a subscriber? You can sign up here.
Instead, Musk spent much of the week arguing with lawyers (including his own!), changing his story, an …
Read the full story at The Verge.

Er hat Monate damit verbracht, zu behaupten, OpenAI habe „eine gemeinnützige Organisation gestohlen“ und er sei die eigentliche treibende Kraft hinter einem der derzeit wichtigsten Technologieunternehmen. Alles deutet darauf hin, dass er seinen Prozess gegen das Unternehmen nicht gewinnen wird, aber er kämpft trotzdem dagegen ...

title: Christian content creators are outsourcing AI slop to gig workers on Fiverr
description:
In the beginning, platforms like Fiverr were places where people could hire freelancers to do specialized creative labor using skills that took years to develop. In the age of generative AI, though, many of these gig workers have embraced the technology in order to meet clients' demands. These workers' profiles emphasize that they can quickly (and cheaply) whip up images and videos of just about anything. But often, what their clients are looking for are dramatic animations inspired by the Christian Bible.
On TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, and Facebook it is very easy to stumble across AI-generated clips that retell stories from the Bible. Lik …
Read the full story at The Verge.
descriptionDE: Anfangs waren Plattformen wie Fiverr Orte, an denen Menschen Freiberufler einstellen konnten, die spezialisierte kreative Arbeit leisteten und dabei Fähigkeiten nutzten, deren Entwicklung Jahre dauerte. Im Zeitalter der generativen KI haben sich jedoch viele dieser Gig-Worker die Technologie zu eigen gemacht, um den Anforderungen der Kunden gerecht zu werden. Diese funktionieren...
titleDE: Christliche Content-Ersteller lagern die KI-Arbeit an Gig-Worker auf Fiverr aus

title: Microsoft wants lawyers to trust its new AI agent in Word documents
description:
Microsoft is launching a new AI agent inside Word that's specifically designed for legal teams. Legal Agent handles document edits, negotiation history, and complex documents to help legal teams handle tasks like reviewing contracts.
"Instead of relying on general AI models to interpret commands, the agent follows structured workflows shaped by real legal practice, managing clearly defined, repeatable tasks like reviewing contracts clause by clause against a playbook," explains Sumit Chauhan, corporate vice president of Microsoft's Office Product Group. 
The Legal Agent can work with existing documents that have tracked changes, and analyz …
Read the full story at The Verge.
descriptionDE: Microsoft führt einen neuen KI-Agenten in Word ein, der speziell für Rechtsteams entwickelt wurde. Legal Agent kümmert sich um Dokumentbearbeitungen, den Verhandlungsverlauf und komplexe Dokumente, um Rechtsteams bei der Bewältigung von Aufgaben wie der Überprüfung von Verträgen zu unterstützen. „Anstatt sich bei der Interpretation von Befehlen auf allgemeine KI-Modelle zu verlassen, ...
titleDE: Microsoft möchte, dass Anwälte seinem neuen KI-Agenten in Word-Dokumenten vertrauen

But it isn’t doing it alone; for a month starting this week, it will…

It blocks porn, which experts in network security say marks the first time a US cell plan has used network-level blocking for such content that can’t be turned off even by adult account owners. It’s also rolling out a filter…

title: The craziest part of Musk v. Altman happened while the jury was out of the room
description:
Okay, I am not a lawyer so I only understood about half of what just happened. But I am fairly sure, given the context, that Elon Musk's lawyers may have just fucked up big.
Jared "James Brickhouse" Birchall, Musk's finance guy and all-around fixer, took the stand after Musk today. Most of his testimony was dull and seemed to exist primarily to get some documents read into the record, which sucks but is a normal part of sitting through trials. But at the very end of his boring testimony something interesting happened. I believe we all got a surprise, something that rarely happens in courtrooms.
The lawyer conducting his direct examination  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
descriptionDE: Okay, ich bin kein Anwalt, also habe ich nur etwa die Hälfte von dem verstanden, was gerade passiert ist. Angesichts des Kontexts bin ich mir jedoch ziemlich sicher, dass die Anwälte von Elon Musk einfach einen großen Fehler gemacht haben. Jared „James Brickhouse“ Birchall, Musks Finanzmann und Alleskönner, trat heute nach Musk in den Zeugenstand. Die meisten seiner Zeugnisse...
titleDE: Der verrückteste Teil von Musk vs. Altman ereignete sich, während die Jury nicht im Saal war

title: Elon Musk confirms xAI used OpenAI’s models to train Grok
description:
In a federal courtroom in California on Thursday, Elon Musk testified that his own AI startup, xAI, has used OpenAI's models to improve its own. 
The matter at question is model distillation, a common industry practice by which one larger AI model acts as a "teacher" of sorts to pass on knowledge to a smaller AI model, the "student." Although it's often used legitimately within companies using one of their own AI models to train another, it's also a practice that's sometimes used by smaller AI labs to try to get their models to mimic the performance of a larger competitor's model. 
Asked on the stand whether he knew what model distillation …
Read the full story at The Verge.
descriptionDE: In einem Bundesgerichtssaal in Kalifornien sagte Elon Musk am Donnerstag aus, dass sein eigenes KI-Startup xAI die Modelle von OpenAI genutzt habe, um seine eigenen zu verbessern. Es geht um die Modelldestillation, eine gängige Branchenpraxis, bei der ein größeres KI-Modell als eine Art „Lehrer“ fungiert, um Wissen an ... weiterzugeben.
titleDE: Elon Musk bestätigt, dass xAI die Modelle von OpenAI verwendet hat, um Grok zu trainieren

title: Elon Musk testifies that xAI trained Grok on OpenAI models
description: "Distillation" is a hot topic as frontier labs try to prevent smaller competitors from copying their models.

titleDE: Elon Musk bezeugt, dass xAI Grok auf OpenAI-Modellen trainiert hat

title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
description:
Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead. 
The trial began with jury selection on April 27th, before Elon Musk took the stand on Tuesday as the first witness called, portraying his interest in founding OpenAI as an effort to help save humanity, before returning to the stand on Wednesday, and again for a third day of testimony on Thursday.
Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and cofounder Greg Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI says that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.
In his lawsuit, Musk is asking for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation. Musk has also demanded that OpenAI’s nonprofit receive up to $150 billion in damages he’s asking for if he wins the case.
Here’s all the latest on the trial between Musk and Altman:
OpenAI Tesla receipts and other Musk v. Altman documents.
			
All the evidence revealed so far in Musk v. Altman
			
Here’s how Gabe Newell and Hideo Kojima ended up in the Musk v. Altman evidence.
			
The craziest part of Musk v. Altman happened while the jury was out of the room
			
Jury is being dismissed early so YGR can deal with an objection to Birchall’s testimony.
			
Birchall is actually very funny outside of court? Good for him.
			
We are now hearing about the pause in quarterly donations.
			
We’re back.
			
Second break of the day.
			
Birchall cross.
			
Elon Musk confirms xAI used OpenAI’s models to train Grok
			
Birchall has just been asked about the four Teslas.
			
Birchall testifies about Musk’s contributions to OpenAI.
			
A woman in the gallery has lowered a sleep mask over her eyes and is attempting to sleep.
			
Musk steps down. He may be recalled.
			
We are on re-cross. Musk is getting testy again.
			
The Microsoft investment comes back up.
			
And we’re back.
			
We’re in break — and I just checked out something interesting.
			
Elon Musk’s robot army definitely will not kill you.
			
Musk insists he wasn’t kneecapping OpenAI.
			
Musk seems notably more subdued today.
			
“At least change the name,” Musk says he told Altman.
			
Elon Musk v. Capitalism.
			
An “ongoing conversation” around open source.
			
We’re still talking about whether Musk read the term sheet.
			
The jurors have been seated.
			
Musk has just entered the courtroom.
			
“Issues of extinction are excluded.”
			
Good morning!
			
Elon Musk’s worst enemy in court is Elon Musk
			
Freedom!
			
Unfortunately we will not be talking about safety details of any specific product.
			
The jury is leaving for the day. “I suspect it’s a nice day out there,” YGR says.
			
Mechahitler might be a bad look for the AI safety defender.
			
Musk’s broader AI safety commitment (or lack thereof) comes up.
			
This is so testy.
			
Did Musk even read the OpenAI term sheet?
			
Musk asked Shivon Zilis to stay ‘close and friendly’ with OpenAI to keep info flowing.
			
Musk says xAI probably won’t be the first to get to AGI.
			
We’re back from a break, talking about SpaceX and xAI.
			
Don’t worry about Tesla’s robot army!
			
“You mostly do unfair questions.”
			
“It’s a free country.”
			
“Will you answer my question?”
			
Musk’s desire for control comes up again.
			
“This is a hypothetical.”
			
Did Musk initially envision OpenAI as a corporation?
			
Musk is being combative on cross already
			
“I did say that I would commit up to a billion dollars, yes.”
			
Is Tesla really not working on AGI?
			
Musk is returning to the stand.
			
At times, being a judge is much like being a kindergarden teacher.
			
We’re on a break.
			
“I mean, all due respect to Microsoft, do you really want Microsoft controlling digital superintelligence?”
			
“What’s going on here this is a bait and switch.”
			
A Musk-Altman spat about Microsoft.
			
Musk really cannot help himself.
			
“Capped profit” wasn’t an issue, even when Microsoft got involved.
			
“Tesla is not pursuing AGI.”
			
Musk is more on his game today.
			
“After I received these reassurances that OpenAI would continue to be a non-profit I continued to donate over $10 million.”
			
“I actually was a fool who provided free funding for them to create a startup.”
			
More discussion of who would own OpenAI.
			
“I don’t lose my temper,” says Elon Musk.
			
“2017 was a hard year, and we’ve made mistakes.”
			
“I formed many for-profit tech companies, and could have done so with OAI.”
			
“Crystal clear focus.”
			
Sam Altman has just entered the room, right ahead of the jury.
			
A member of the public just got dressed down by YGR about taking photos.
			
Musk v Altman et al. is back in session.
			
In naming OpenAI, Elon Musk worried anything related to the Turing Test could mean bad PR.
			
Elon Musk appeared more petty than prepared
			
That’s a wrap!
			
YGR scolds OpenAI for taking inconsistent positions on the origin of its name.
			
Elon Musk tells the jury that all he wants to do is save humanity
			
Arguments over ownership.
			
Apparently OpenAI could have had an ICO.
			
“I was not averse to a small for-profit,” Musk says.
			
We’re reading emails between Musk and Jensen Huang.
			
Musk says nonprofit was non-negotiable for OpenAI.
			
We’re at the founding of OpenAI.
			
Musk says he would have created something like OpenAI on his own.
			
Musk recalls meeting Sam Altman.
			
Sam Altman left during a break, but Elon Musk’s lawyer didn’t notice.
			
“Here we are in 2026 and AI is scary smart.”
			
“I have extreme concerns about AI,” says Musk.
			
AI will be as smart as ‘any human as soon as next year.’
			
Musk claims he has time for SpaceX, Tesla, Neuralink, and the Boring Company because he works a lot.
			
Musk is telling the jury he (co)founded Tesla.
			
Neuralink’s long-term goal is… AI?
			
“There need to be things that people are excited about that make life worth living … being out there among the stars can excite everyone.”
			
A little Musk biography.
			
Elon Musk, looking funereal in a black suit with a black tie, says “it’s not okay to steal a charity.”
			
Elon Musk takes the stand in high-profile trial against OpenAI
			
We are back from a break.
			
Elon Musk will be the first witness in Musk v. Altman.
			
“Microsoft unlocked with OpenAI a virtuous cycle.”
			
Microsoft enters the chat.
			
“We are here because Mr Musk didn’t get his way at OpenAI.”
			
“[Musk] demanded control, he demanded the ability to make all the decisions without regard to the other founders.”
			
OpenAI lawyers argue that Elon was right in the middle of discussions about a for-profit pivot.
			
“Musk was furious that OpenAI succeeded.”
			
OpenAI: Musk’s lawsuit is a “pageant of hypocrisy.”
			
Sam Altman’s “related party conflicted transactions” are how he made money on OpenAI, Molo says.
			
Technical difficulties.
			
OpenAI is like a museum store that has looted the Picassos and pocketed the profits.
			
AGI might be out of fashion in the AI world, but it will be at the center of this trial
			
“The defendants in this case stole a charity.”
			
Musk and Altman go to court
			
Good morning from the Musk v Altman line outside the courtroom.
			
Jury selection in Musk v. Altman: ‘People don’t like him’
			
We have a jury.
			
Elon Musk’s lawyer tried to get some jurors thrown out for disliking Musk.
			
Apparently things are exciting outside.
			
We have gone through the first 20 potential jurors.
			
Voir dire has begun.
			
The Elon Musk vs. OpenAI trial starts today.
			
Elon Musk drops fraud claims against OpenAI and Sam Altman before trial.
			
Musk vs. Altman is here, and it’s going to get messy
			
Elon Musk is about to be a very busy boy!
			
‘Sideshow’ concerns and billionaire dreams: What I learned from Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI
			
Elon Musk’s xAI is suing OpenAI and Apple
			
Inside Elon Musk’s messy breakup with OpenAI
			
Elon Musk is suing OpenAI and Sam Altman again
descriptionDE: Sam Altman und Elon Musk stehen sich in einem hochriskanten Prozess gegenüber, der die Zukunft von OpenAI und seinem bekanntesten Produkt, ChatGPT, verändern könnte. Im Jahr 2024 reichte Musk eine Klage ein, in der er OpenAI beschuldigte, seine Gründungsmission, KI zum Nutzen der Menschheit zu entwickeln, aufgegeben und den Fokus auf die Steigerung der Gewinne verlagert zu haben ...
titleDE: Live-Updates vom Gerichtsstreit zwischen Elon Musk und Sam Altman über die Zukunft von OpenAI

title: Meta is running get-rich-quick ads for its AI tools
description:
Manus, an AI company Meta acquired for $2 billion last year is running ads promising quick, easy money with AI: Find local businesses without websites or with bad websites, have AI build them one, then call them up and sell it to them.
As part of the campaign, Manus was paying content creators to build out Instagram, YouTube, and TikTok accounts that promote its AI product as an easy, lucrative gig. (The creators' TikTok accounts were taken down after The Verge inquired about them.) Some of these videos would also appear as official ads for Manus, but the posts on the paid creator accounts themselves often obscured their ties to the company …
Read the full story at The Verge.
descriptionDE: Manus, ein KI-Unternehmen, das Meta letztes Jahr für 2 Milliarden US-Dollar übernommen hat, schaltet Anzeigen, die schnelles und einfaches Geld mit KI versprechen: Finden Sie lokale Unternehmen ohne oder mit schlechten Websites, lassen Sie sie von KI erstellen, rufen Sie sie dann an und verkaufen Sie sie ihnen. Im Rahmen der Kampagne bezahlte Manus Content-Ersteller dafür,...
titleDE: Meta schaltet für seine KI-Tools Werbung, um schnell reich zu werden

title: Salesforce is crowdsourcing its AI roadmap — with customers
description: Salesforce lets its customers lead its product roadmap with the thinking that if one enterprise customer has a problem, the others likely do too.
descriptionDE: Salesforce lässt seine Kunden seine Produkt-Roadmap mit der Überlegung leiten, dass, wenn ein Unternehmenskunde ein Problem hat, die anderen wahrscheinlich auch ein Problem haben.
titleDE: Salesforce betreibt Crowdsourcing seiner KI-Roadmap – mit Kunden

title: Gemini is rolling out to cars with Google built-in
description:
Here’s an early look at the new Gemini assistant on a vehicle infotainment system. | Image: Google	

Google is preparing to update vehicles that have Google built-in with its Gemini AI assistant.  This will be an upgrade from the current Google Assistant according to Google's announcement, and promises to provide an improved experience for natural conversations, fetching vehicle-specific information, settings adjustments, and more.
"When cars with Google built-in first hit the road in 2020, we made a commitment that your car will get better over time," Google senior product manager Alankar Agnihotri said in the announcement. "That means that Gemini is coming not only to new cars, but also to existing ones through a software update."
The a …
Read the full story at The Verge.
descriptionDE: Hier ist ein erster Blick auf den neuen Gemini-Assistenten in einem Fahrzeug-Infotainmentsystem. | Bild: Google Google bereitet sich darauf vor, Fahrzeuge mit integriertem Google-System mit dem KI-Assistenten Gemini zu aktualisieren. Dies wird laut Googles Ankündigung ein Upgrade des aktuellen Google Assistant sein und verspricht...
titleDE: Gemini wird mit integriertem Google in Autos eingeführt

title: This startup’s new mechanistic interpretability tool lets you debug LLMs
description: The San Francisco–based startup Goodfire just released a new tool, called Silico, that lets researchers and engineers peer inside an AI model and adjust its parameters—the settings that determine a model’s behavior—during training. This could give model makers more fine-grained control over how this technology is built than was once thought possible. Goodfire claims Silico…
descriptionDE: Das in San Francisco ansässige Startup Goodfire hat gerade ein neues Tool namens Silico veröffentlicht, mit dem Forscher und Ingenieure einen Blick in ein KI-Modell werfen und dessen Parameter – die Einstellungen, die das Verhalten eines Modells bestimmen – während des Trainings anpassen können. Dies könnte Modellbauern eine genauere Kontrolle darüber geben, wie dies ...
titleDE: Mit dem neuen mechanistischen Interpretierbarkeitstool dieses Startups können Sie LLMs debuggen

title: X announces a rebuilt ad platform powered by AI
description: X is rolling out a rebuilt ads platform powered by AI as it works to grow revenue again.
descriptionDE: X führt eine neu gestaltete, auf KI basierende Anzeigenplattform ein, um den Umsatz erneut zu steigern.
titleDE: X kündigt eine neu gestaltete, auf KI basierende Werbeplattform an

title: The Download: the North Pole’s future and humanoid data
description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Digging for clues about the North Pole’s past In the past, getting to the North Pole involved a treacherous trip through ice many meters thick. But last year, a research vessel…
descriptionDE: Dies ist die heutige Ausgabe von The Download, unserem Wochentags-Newsletter, der täglich einen Überblick über die aktuellen Entwicklungen in der Welt der Technologie bietet. Auf der Suche nach Hinweisen auf die Vergangenheit des Nordpols Früher war der Weg zum Nordpol mit einer tückischen Reise durch meterdickes Eis verbunden. Aber letztes Jahr, ein r...
titleDE: Der Download: die Zukunft des Nordpols und humanoide Daten

title: Meta says its business AI now facilitates 10 million conversations a week
description: Meta said over 8 billion advertisers have used at least one of its GenAI tools
descriptionDE: Laut Meta haben über 8 Milliarden Werbetreibende mindestens eines seiner GenAI-Tools verwendet
titleDE: Meta gibt an, dass seine Geschäfts-KI inzwischen 10 Millionen Gespräche pro Woche ermöglicht

title: Introducing Advanced Account Security
description: Introducing Advanced Account Security: phishing-resistant login, stronger recovery, and enhanced protections to safeguard sensitive data and prevent account takeover.
descriptionDE: Einführung der erweiterten Kontosicherheit: Phishing-resistente Anmeldung, stärkere Wiederherstellung und verbesserte Schutzmaßnahmen zum Schutz sensibler Daten und zur Verhinderung von Kontoübernahmen.
titleDE: Einführung der erweiterten Kontosicherheit

title: Where the goblins came from
description: How goblin outputs spread in AI models: timeline, root cause, and fixes behind personality-driven quirks in GPT-5 behavior.
descriptionDE: Wie sich Goblin-Ausgaben in KI-Modellen verbreiten: Zeitleiste, Grundursache und Lösungen für persönlichkeitsbedingte Macken im GPT-5-Verhalten.
titleDE: Woher die Kobolde kamen

title: Building the compute infrastructure for the Intelligence Age
description: OpenAI scales Stargate to build the compute infrastructure powering AGI, adding new data center capacity to meet growing AI demand.
descriptionDE: OpenAI skaliert Stargate, um die Recheninfrastruktur für AGI aufzubauen und fügt neue Rechenzentrumskapazitäten hinzu, um der wachsenden KI-Nachfrage gerecht zu werden.
titleDE: Aufbau der Recheninfrastruktur für das Intelligenzzeitalter

title: Why a recent supply-chain attack singled out security firms Checkmarx and Bitwarden
description: Security firms find themselves especially exposed.
descriptionDE: Besonders gefährdet sehen sich Sicherheitsfirmen.
titleDE: Warum ein kürzlich erfolgter Supply-Chain-Angriff die Sicherheitsfirmen Checkmarx und Bitwarden ins Visier nahm

title: Cybersecurity in the Intelligence Age
description: OpenAI outlines a five-part action plan for strengthening cybersecurity in the Intelligence Age, focused on democratizing AI-powered cyber defense and protecting critical systems.
descriptionDE: OpenAI skizziert einen fünfteiligen Aktionsplan zur Stärkung der Cybersicherheit im Zeitalter der Intelligenz, der sich auf die Demokratisierung der KI-gestützten Cyberabwehr und den Schutz kritischer Systeme konzentriert.
titleDE: Cybersicherheit im Zeitalter der Intelligenz

title: Celebrating 20 years of Google Translate: Fun facts, tips and new features to try
description: Google’s sharing 20 fun facts to celebrate Google Translate turning 20, from its roots as a 2006 AI experiment to supporting almost 250 languages today.
descriptionDE: Google teilt anlässlich des 20-jährigen Jubiläums von Google Translate 20 unterhaltsame Fakten mit – von den Anfängen als KI-Experiment im Jahr 2006 bis hin zur Unterstützung von fast 250 Sprachen heute.
titleDE: Wir feiern 20 Jahre Google Translate: Interessante Fakten, Tipps und neue Funktionen zum Ausprobieren

title: Our commitment to community safety
description: Learn how OpenAI protects community safety in ChatGPT through model safeguards, misuse detection, policy enforcement, and collaboration with safety experts.
descriptionDE: Erfahren Sie, wie OpenAI die Community-Sicherheit in ChatGPT durch Modellschutz, Missbrauchserkennung, Richtliniendurchsetzung und Zusammenarbeit mit Sicherheitsexperten schützt.
titleDE: Unser Engagement für die Sicherheit der Gemeinschaft

title: OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents come to AWS
description: OpenAI GPT models, Codex, and Managed Agents are now available on AWS, enabling enterprises to build secure AI in their AWS environments.
descriptionDE: OpenAI GPT-Modelle, Codex und Managed Agents sind jetzt auf AWS verfügbar und ermöglichen es Unternehmen, sichere KI in ihren AWS-Umgebungen aufzubauen.
titleDE: OpenAI-Modelle, Codex und Managed Agents kommen zu AWS

title: Open source package with 1 million monthly downloads stole user credentials
description: If you're one of millions using element-data, it's time to check for compromise.
descriptionDE: Wenn Sie einer von Millionen sind, die Elementdaten verwenden, ist es an der Zeit, nach Kompromissen zu suchen.
titleDE: Open-Source-Paket mit 1 Million monatlichen Downloads stahl Benutzeranmeldeinformationen

title: OpenAI available at FedRAMP Moderate
description: OpenAI is available at FedRAMP Moderate authorization for ChatGPT Enterprise and the OpenAI API, enabling secure AI adoption for U.S. federal agencies.
descriptionDE: OpenAI ist bei FedRAMP Moderate-Autorisierung für ChatGPT Enterprise und die OpenAI-API verfügbar und ermöglicht so eine sichere KI-Einführung für US-Bundesbehörden.
titleDE: OpenAI verfügbar bei FedRAMP Moderate

title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle
description: Google is bringing back its 5-Day AI Agents Intensive Course with Kaggle and registration is open.
descriptionDE: Google bringt seinen 5-tägigen KI-Agenten-Intensivkurs mit Kaggle zurück und die Registrierung ist geöffnet.
titleDE: Nehmen Sie am neuen AI Agents Vibe Coding-Kurs von Google und Kaggle teil

title: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership
description: OpenAI and Microsoft announce an amended agreement that simplifies the partnership, adds long-term clarity, and supports continued AI innovation at scale.
descriptionDE: OpenAI und Microsoft geben eine geänderte Vereinbarung bekannt, die die Partnerschaft vereinfacht, langfristige Klarheit schafft und kontinuierliche KI-Innovationen in großem Maßstab unterstützt.
titleDE: Die nächste Phase der Microsoft OpenAI-Partnerschaft

title: An open-source spec for orchestration: Symphony
description: Learn how Symphony, an open-source spec for Codex orchestration, turns issue trackers into always-on agent systems—boosting engineering output and reducing context switching.
descriptionDE: Erfahren Sie, wie Symphony, eine Open-Source-Spezifikation für die Codex-Orchestrierung, Issue-Tracker in ständig aktive Agentensysteme verwandelt – wodurch die technische Leistung gesteigert und der Kontextwechsel reduziert wird.
titleDE: Eine Open-Source-Spezifikation für Orchestrierung: Symphony

title: Choco automates food distribution with AI agents
description: How Choco used OpenAI APIs to streamline food distribution, boost productivity, and unlock growth—an in-depth customer story on real-world AI impact.
descriptionDE: Wie Choco OpenAI-APIs nutzte, um die Lebensmittelverteilung zu optimieren, die Produktivität zu steigern und Wachstum zu ermöglichen – eine ausführliche Kundengeschichte über die Auswirkungen von KI in der Praxis.
titleDE: Choco automatisiert die Lebensmittelverteilung mit KI-Agenten

title: Our principles
description: Our mission is to ensure that AGI benefits all of humanity. Sam Altman shares five principles that guide our work.
descriptionDE: Unsere Mission ist es sicherzustellen, dass AGI der gesamten Menschheit zugute kommt. Sam Altman teilt fünf Prinzipien, die unsere Arbeit leiten.
titleDE: Unsere Prinzipien
